channel.title: RSS Aggregator Feed
channel.description: An aggregated feed of Technology-related news

item.title: I replaced my space heater and ceiling fan with one Dyson appliance
item.description: Designed for year-round comfort, the Dyson Hot+Cool HF1 combines quiet operation and simple controls with Dyson's signature bladeless design.

item.title: Brex built its AI agent policy by watching what agents actually do, not by writing rules first
item.description: OpenClaw has become one of the most widely adopted agentic frameworks, but it has yet to prove itself at enterprise scale. Agents need real credentials — API keys, OAuth tokens, service accounts — to work effectively, and Brex found that traditional guardrails couldn't contain what those agents were doing with them.Brex set out to overcome these limitations by building an internal platform it calls CrabTrap. The open-source HTTP/HTTPS proxy intercepts all network traffic, examines policy rules, and uses a LLM-as-a-judge to decide whether agent requests should be approved or denied. “What we no...

item.title: ICE Is Using Data Broker Tools to ‘Identify Unaccompanied Minors’ and ‘Fraud’
item.description: A newly renewed, $25 million-per-year contract with a subsidiary of Thompson Reuters further expands the power of ICE under the Trump administration.

item.title: How Apple’s big lawsuit could disrupt OpenAI’s IPO plans
item.description: Apple filed a trade secrets lawsuit against OpenAI last Friday, and it’s not messing around. The complaint alleges a pattern of misconduct reaching all the way up to OpenAI’s chief hardware officer and claims more than 400 former Apple employees now work at the company. OpenAI’s response so far has been carefully hedged, and the timing couldn’t be worse with the company reportedly eyeing an IPO […]...

item.title: Apple’s plot to crush OpenAI
item.description: Apple is suing OpenAI. The complaint is readable and intense, as these things often are, though many experts seem to think many of the allegations are just the ways things are done. So what does Apple really want here, and why is it picking such a public fight with OpenAI? On this episode of The […]

item.title: ‘The Odyssey’ Backlash Failed Tremendously
item.description: For all the hysteria over “woke” casting and ahistorical choices, Christopher Nolan’s epic is on track to make $200 million globally during its opening weekend.

item.title: A bug in AWS has caused some customers' bills to spike from a few cents to billions of dollars
item.description: "The displayed billing estimates do not reflect actual usage and charges," Amazon said.

item.title: Apple sends legal letters to dozens of OpenAI defectors, report says
item.description: Apple appears to believe that additional former employees have shared company information with OpenAI.

item.title: Bethesda details what's next for Fallout and its other franchises amid Xbox cuts
item.description: Obsidian is working on a new Fallout game, while Fallout 3 and New Vegas remasters are on the way.

item.title: 'Whoever came up with this is a massive idiot': LG's gaming monitors and TVs are facing a user revolt, due to seemingly installing adware on PCs — and telling you to warn guests they may be recorded by AI features, to comply with 'wiretapping' laws
item.description: LG's terms and conditions say you need to get consent from visitors because its TVs can listen to you.

item.title: A planet with an Earth-like atmosphere has been found
item.description: A new study in Science indicates a nearby exoplanet may have an atmosphere like ours, moving us closer to finding life-sustaining worlds.

item.title: Too hot to cook? Save on delivery with DoorDash e-gift cards and unlock $20 in free food.
item.description: DoorDash gift cards of $50, $100, and $200 values are on sale at Best Buy as of July 17.

item.title: Asus’ top-end 4K QD-OLED gaming monitor is $400 off
item.description: The Asus ROG Swift 32-inch 4K QD-OLED gaming monitor has almost everything I want in a high-end gaming monitor, most notably a QD-OLED panel for inky black levels and vivid colors that go beyond what’s possible on an LCD screen. Amazon, Best Buy, and B&H Photo have this model on sale for $899.99 (usually $1,299.99). […]

item.title: Cadences World Cup travel pods show team spirit without being cringe — on sale now
item.description: Cadence dropped limited-edition, team-inspired magnetic travel capsules just in time for the World Cup final this weekend.

item.title: Soldes : Boulanger propose un écran PC gamer Full HD de 27″ et 240 Hz à petit prix
item.description: L'Iiyama G-Master Red Eagle 27" est un écran qui prouve qu'il n'y a pas besoin de dépenser des fortunes pour se faire plaisir. Surtout qu'il est soldé à 139,95 euros au lieu de 159,99 euros sur Boulanger.

item.title: Musk’s SpaceX Starship launch suddenly aborted in what would have been it’s 13th flight
item.description: The world’s biggest rocket was seconds away from blast off. SpaceX’s mega Starship rocket came within a second or so from blasting off on a test flight Thursday, but some of the engines failed to ignite, triggering a launch abort amid billowing clouds of smoke and vapor.Elon Musk, the company’s founder and CEO, said two engines will be replaced “to be confident of a good flight” before sending Starship from Texas on a space-skimming journey halfway around the world. It will be the 13th flight for Starship, which at 407 feet (124 meters) tall with 33 main engines is the world’s biggest and most...

item.title: Kodak's new point-and-shoot 35mm film camera comes in 7 vibrant colors, costs just $35, and brings back one of my favorite pieces of camera design
item.description: Reminiscent of the classic Olympus Mju, the new 35mm film camera costs just $35.

item.title: Le Nubia Z70 Ultra retiré du marché français par l’ANFR à cause d’un DAS trop élevé
item.description: L'ANFR vient de d'indiquer le retrait du marché français et le rappel du Nubia Z70 Ultra. Le smartphone haut de gamme dépasse le DAS limite des autorités sanitaires européennes. Si vous en possédez un, voici ce que cela change.

item.title: FBI arrests man accused of using Steam games to drain victims’ crypto wallets
item.description: Prosecutors accused 21-year-old student Zyaire Wilkins of publishing on Steam several fake video games that contained malware, infecting thousands of victims, and stealing crypto from some of them.

item.title: Moonshot reveals new AI model, and it's a big surprise — here's why Kimi K3 is a threat to the likes of OpenAI
item.description: Kimi K3 model is a threat to the likes of OpenAI for two key reasons: its open nature, and apparently sterling coding skills.

item.title: Nearly 736,000 Pillsbury bread rolls recalled over possible glass contamination
item.description: If your restaurant, cafeteria, or kitchen serves Pillsbury frozen rolls, check your inventory now after a major FDA recall. General Mills is issuing a major recall for some of its Pillsbury roll products. At present, at least 736,000 Pillsbury bread rolls have been pulled from shelves due to possible glass contamination.

item.title: Cyclospora outbreak map: Don’t eat shredded lettuce at Taco Bell in these states, CDC warns
item.description: A new update and interactive map from the agency traces the ongoing outbreak of cyclosporiasis, which is caused by a food parasite, to a Taco Bell ingredient. There’s been a new update from the Centers for Disease Control and Prevention (CDC) on the ongoing Cyclospora outbreak affecting people in five states. Now, the agency is warning people to avoid a specific ingredient in Taco Bell products in those states. Here’s what you need to know....

item.title: Parents want safer phones for kids. These companies are answering the call.
item.description: As parents look for alternatives to unrestricted smartphones, a growing number of companies are building phones designed specifically for kids, from feature-limited mobile devices to minimalist home phones.

item.title: Apple and Google ordered by San Francisco attorney to take action against 'nudify' apps
item.description: Google and Apple were sent cease-and-desist letters regarding 13 apps on their respective stores.

item.title: 'You know who isn’t the hero? The killer of physical' — Angry PlayStation fans are flocking to the new Wolverine trailer
item.description: PlayStation fans angry with Sony's decision to end physical discs have descended on the latest Marvel's Wolverine to make their thoughts known.
